title: AI News Daily — July 18, 2026
summary: Google missed its own rebuilt-model deadline for a third time, leaving Gemini 3.5 Pro in limbo while GPT-5.6 and Claude keep setting the pace. On the geopolitical side, Xi Jinping made his first-ever appearance at China's World AI Conference to launch a new global AI governance body, and NVIDIA anchored a $6.2 billion state-backed AI factory in Japan. DeepMind opened a bioresilience program to get ahead of AI-enabled biothreats, and xAI's legal troubles over Grok-generated child sexual abuse material widened.
